---
title: "Interactive Visualizations"
author: "Junwei Quan"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
---

# Interactive Visualizations

This page provides interactive visualizations for the final project on University of Toronto machine-learning publications in OpenAlex. The figures are designed to complement the written report by allowing users to explore annual trends, publication-level citation patterns, and model interpretation results.

```{python}
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

DATA_DIR = Path("data")

df = pd.read_parquet(DATA_DIR / "openalex_ml_works_clean.parquet")

trend = (
    df.groupby("publication_year", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        n_works=("id", "nunique"),
        n_oa=("is_oa", lambda x: (x == "OA").sum()),
        n_international=("international", lambda x: (x == "Yes").sum()),
    )
    .rename(columns={"publication_year": "year"})
    .sort_values("year")
)

trend["Open-access share"] = trend["n_oa"] / trend["n_works"]
trend["International-collaboration share"] = trend["n_international"] / trend["n_works"]
```

## Annual publication output

```{python}
fig = px.line(
    trend,
    x="year",
    y="n_works",
    markers=True,
    labels={
        "year": "Publication year",
        "n_works": "Number of works",
    },
    title="Annual number of keyword-defined machine-learning works",
)

fig.update_layout(
    hovermode="x unified",
)

fig.show()
```

**Figure 1.** This interactive line chart shows the annual number of keyword-defined machine-learning works affiliated with the University of Toronto from 2015 to 2025.

## Open access and international collaboration

```{python}
trend_long = trend.melt(
    id_vars="year",
    value_vars=["Open-access share", "International-collaboration share"],
    var_name="Metric",
    value_name="Share",
)

fig = px.line(
    trend_long,
    x="year",
    y="Share",
    color="Metric",
    markers=True,
    labels={
        "year": "Publication year",
        "Share": "Share of works",
    },
    title="Open access and international collaboration over time",
)

fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".0%")
fig.update_layout(
    hovermode="x unified",
)

fig.show()
```

**Figure 2.** This interactive chart compares open-access share and international-collaboration share over time. Users can click the legend to show or hide either metric.

## Citation explorer

```{python}
plot_df = df.copy()

plot_df["short_title"] = (
    plot_df["title"]
    .fillna("Untitled")
    .astype(str)
    .str.slice(0, 100)
)

plot_df["venue"] = plot_df["venue"].fillna("Unknown")
plot_df["primary_topic_name"] = plot_df["primary_topic_name"].fillna("Unknown")

# Sampling keeps the interactive plot responsive if the dataset is large.
if len(plot_df) > 5000:
    plot_df = plot_df.sample(5000, random_state=370)

fig = px.scatter(
    plot_df,
    x="authors_count",
    y="log_cites_per_year",
    color="is_oa",
    hover_name="short_title",
    hover_data={
        "publication_year": True,
        "venue": True,
        "primary_topic_name": True,
        "authors_count": True,
        "cited_by_count": True,
        "log_cites_per_year": ":.3f",
        "is_oa": True,
    },
    labels={
        "authors_count": "Number of authors",
        "log_cites_per_year": "log(1 + citations per year)",
        "is_oa": "Open-access status",
    },
    title="Team size and citation impact",
)

fig.update_traces(marker={"opacity": 0.55})
fig.show()
```

**Figure 3.** This publication-level citation explorer shows the relationship between team size and citation impact. Hover text provides paper-level metadata including title, year, venue, topic, and citation count.

## Model variable importance

```{python}
importance = pd.read_csv("tables/variable_importance.csv")

importance_top = (
    importance
    .sort_values("importance_mean", ascending=False)
    .head(15)
    .sort_values("importance_mean", ascending=True)
)

fig = px.bar(
    importance_top,
    x="importance_mean",
    y="feature",
    orientation="h",
    error_x="importance_sd",
    labels={
        "importance_mean": "Permutation importance",
        "importance_sd": "Standard deviation",
        "feature": "Feature",
    },
    title="Top predictors from the best-performing citation model",
)

fig.show()
```

**Figure 4.** This interactive bar chart shows permutation importance from the best-performing XGBoost model. Larger values indicate that shuffling the feature caused a greater decrease in predictive performance.
